1: David's son and successor to his throne. From shalowm; peaceful; Shelomah, David's successor.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
2: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
3: To take. A primitive root; to take.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Evenness, uprightness, equity. From yashar; evenness, i.e. prosperity or concord; also straightness, i.e. rectitude.
4: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Craftiness, prudence. Feminine of orem; trickery; or discretion.
Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.
Purpose, discretion, device. From zamam; a plan, usually evil, sometimes good.
A boy, lad, youth, retainer. From na'ar; a boy, from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also, a girl.
5: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
A learning, teaching. From laqach; properly, something received, i.e. instruction; also inveiglement.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
A primitive root; to erect, i.e. Create; by extension, to procure, especially by purchase; by implication to own.
Direction, counsel. Or tachbuwlah; from chabal as denominative from chebel; properly, steerage, i.e. guidance or a plan.
6: To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Satire, a mocking poem. From luwts; an aphorism; also a satire.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
A riddle, an enigmatic, perplexing saying or question. From chuwd; a puzzle, hence, a trick, conundrum, sententious maxim.
7: A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Beginning, chief. From the same as ro'sh; the first, in place, time, order or rank.
Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
To despise. A primitive root; to disrespect.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
8: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
A primitive root; properly, to pound, i.e. Smite; by implication to disperse; also, to thrust off, down, out or upon.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
A mother. A primitive word; a mother; in a wide sense (like 'ab).
9: They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
A wreath. From lavah; something attached, i.e. A wreath.
Favor, grace. From chanan; graciousness, i.e. Subjective or objective.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A necklace, neck pendant. From anaq; a necklace.
Neck. Feminine plural from garar; the throat.
10: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Sinful, sinners. Intensively from chata'; a criminal, or one accounted guilty.
A primitive root; to open, i.e. Be roomy; usually figuratively to be simple or delude.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To be willing, to consent. A primitive root; to breathe after, i.e. to be acquiescent.
11: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
To lie in wait. A primitive root; to lurk.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
Clean, free from, exempt. Or naqiyi,; from naqah; innocent.
Out of favor. From chen; gratis, i.e. Devoid of cost, reason or advantage.
12: To swallow down, swallow up, engulf. A primitive root; to make away with; generally, to destroy.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
Complete, sound. From tamam; entire; also integrity, truth.
To come or go down, descend.
A pit, cistern, well. From buwr; a pit hole.
13: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Precious, rare, splendid, weighty. From yaqar; valuable.
Wealth, sufficiency. From the same as huwn in the sense of 'own; wealth; by implication, enough.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
A prey, spoil, plunder, booty. From shalal; booty.
14: To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Or goral; from an unused root meaning to be rough; properly, a pebble, i.e. A lot; figuratively, a portion or destiny.
Midst. From an unused root meaning to sever; a bisection, i.e. the centre.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Bag, purse. A form for kowc; a cup; also a bag for money or weights.
15: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
To withhold, hold back. A primitive root; to debar from benefit or injury.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
Path, pathway. Or nthiybah; or nthibah; from an unused root meaning to tramp; a track.
16: Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
To run. A primitive root; to run.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To hasten. A primitive root; properly, to be liquid or flow easily, i.e. to hurry; often used adverbially, promptly.
To pour out, pour. A primitive root; to spill forth; also to expend; intensively, to sprawl out.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
17: Out of favor. From chen; gratis, i.e. Devoid of cost, reason or advantage.
To scatter, fan, winnow. A primitive root; to toss about; by implication, to diffuse, winnow.
A net. From yarash; a net.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Owner, lord. From ba'al; a master; hence, a husband, or owner.
Wing, extremity. From kanaph; an edge or extremity; specifically a wing, a flap, a quarter, a pinnacle.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
18: They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To lie in wait. A primitive root; to lurk.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
19: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To cut off, break off, gain by violence. A primitive root to break off, i.e. plunder; figuratively, to finish, or stop.
Gain made by violence, unjust gain, profit. From batsa'; plunder; by extension, gain.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Owner, lord. From ba'al; a master; hence, a husband, or owner.
20: To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A broad open place, plaza. Or rchowb; from rachab; a width, i.e. avenue or area.
21: Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A primitive root; to make a loud sound like Engl. hum); by implication, to be in great commotion or tumult, to rage, war, moan, clamor.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Opening, doorway, entrance. From pathach; an opening, i.e. Door or entrance way.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
22: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
When?. From an unused root meaning to extend; properly, extent; but used only adverbially, when.
Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.
A primitive root; properly, to make mouths at, i.e. To scoff; hence to interpret, or intercede.
To desire, take pleasure in. A primitive root; to delight in.
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
23: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To flow, spring, bubble up. A primitive root; to gush forth; figuratively, to utter; specifically, to emit.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.

24: From an unused root meaning to pay attention; properly, heed; by implication, purpose; used adverbially to indicate the reason or cause.
To refuse. A primitive root; to refuse.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To incline (ears), attend. A primitive root; to prick up the ears, i.e. Hearken.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
25: A primitive root; to loosen; by implication, to expose, dismiss; figuratively, absolve, begin.

Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

26: To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
Distress, calamity. From the same as 'uwd; oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.
To mock, deride, stammer. A primitive root; to deride; by implication to speak unintelligibly.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.

A devastating storm. From sha'ah; a tempest.
Distress, calamity. From the same as 'uwd; oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.

A storm wind. From cuwph; a hurricane.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
Constraint, distress. Or tsuwqah; from tsuwq; a strait, i.e. distress.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
29: Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.

To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.

Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.

A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
A contest, quarrel. A form of madown.
15: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Distress, calamity. From the same as 'uwd; oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Suddenness, suddenly. Or pith.om; from petha'; instantly.
From an unused root meaning to open; a wink, i.e. Moment (with or without preposition) adverbially, quickly or unexpectedly).
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
From rapha'; properly, curative, i.e. Literally a medicine, or a cure; figuratively deliverance, or placidity.
16: Masculine shishshah; a primitive number; six (see suws) beyond five or the fingers of the hand); as ord. Sixth.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
Or shibrah; from shaba'; a primitive cardinal number; seven; also seven times; by implication, a week; by extension, an indefinite number.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
17: To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
To pour out, pour. A primitive root; to spill forth; also to expend; intensively, to sprawl out.
Clean, free from, exempt. Or naqiyi,; from naqah; innocent.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
18: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Thought, device. Or machashebeth; from chashab; a contrivance, i.e. a texture, machine, or intention, plan.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
To run. A primitive root; to run.
To hasten. A primitive root; properly, to be liquid or flow easily, i.e. to hurry; often used adverbially, promptly.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
19: Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.
A witness. Contracted from uwd; concretely, a witness; abstractly, testimony; specifically, a recorder, i.e. Prince.
A lie, falsehood, deceptive thing. From kazab; falsehood; literally or figuratively.
To breathe, blow. A primitive root; to puff, i.e. Blow with the breath or air; hence, to fan, to utter, to kindle, to scoff.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
A contest, quarrel. A form of madown.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
20: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To watch, guard, keep. A primitive root; to guard, in a good sense Or a bad one.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Commandment. From tsavah; a command, whether human or divine.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
A primitive root; properly, to pound, i.e. Smite; by implication to disperse; also, to thrust off, down, out or upon.
A mother. A primitive word; a mother; in a wide sense (like 'ab).
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
21: To bind, league together, conspire. A primitive root: to tie, physically or mentally.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To bind (around or upon). A primitive root; to lace fast.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Neck. Feminine plural from garar; the throat.
22: To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
To lead, guide. A primitive root; to guide; by implication, to transport.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Arise, be awake, watch. A primitive root (compare yaqats); to awake.
To muse, complain, talk (of). A primitive root; to ponder, i.e. converse or utter.
23: Commandment. From tsavah; a command, whether human or divine.
A lamp. Or nir; also neyr; or ner; or nerah; from a primitive root properly, meaning to glisten; a lamp or light.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
24: To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Smoothness, flattery, an allotment. Feminine of cheleq; properly, smoothness; figuratively, flattery; also an allotment.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
Foreign, alien. From neker; strange, in a variety of degrees and applications.
25: Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To desire, take pleasure in. A primitive root; to delight in.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Eyelid. From uwph; an eyelash; figuratively, morning ray.
26: From ad with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
To commit fornication, be a harlot. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to commit idolatry.
From karar; a circle, i.e. a circumjacent tract or region, expec. The Ghor or valley of the Jordan; also a loaf; also a talent (round) coin).
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To hunt. A primitive root; to lie alongside; by implication, to catch an animal; to victual.
Precious, rare, splendid, weighty. From yaqar; valuable.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
27: Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To snatch up. A primitive root; to lay hold of; especially to pick up fire.
Bosom. Or cheq; and chowq; from an unused root, apparently meaning to inclose; the bosom.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
A covering, clothing, treachery, pillage. From bagad; a covering, i.e. Clothing; also treachery or pillage.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To burn. A primitive root; to be on fire.
28: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Coal. Or gacheleth; from an unused root meaning to glow or kindle; an ember.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To burn, scorch, brand. A primitive root; properly, to prick or penetrate; hence, to blister.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
29: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Friend, companion, fellow. Or reya2; from ra'ah; an associate.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
30: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To despise. A primitive root; to disrespect.
A thief. From ganab; a stealer.
To steal. A primitive root; to thieve; by implication, to deceive.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To be hungry. A primitive root; to hunger.
31: A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
Sevenfold, seven times. Dual of sheba'; seven- times.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Wealth, sufficiency. From the same as huwn in the sense of 'own; wealth; by implication, enough.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
32: To commit adultery. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to apostatize.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Needy, lacking, in want of. From chacer; lacking; hence, without.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Perhaps to go to ruin. A primitive root; to decay, i.e. ruin.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
33: A stroke, plague, mark. From naga'; a blow; also a spot.
Ignominy, dishonor. From qalah; disgrace; the pudenda.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To stroke, rub, to erase, to smooth, grease, make fat, to touch, reach to.
34: Ardor, zeal, jealousy. From qana'; jealousy or envy.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
35: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From kaphar; properly, a cover, i.e. a village; bitumen, and the henna plant; figuratively, a redemption-price.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be willing, to consent. A primitive root; to breathe after, i.e. to be acquiescent.
To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
A present, bribe. From shachad; a donation.

Commandment. From tsavah; a command, whether human or divine.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.

Commandment. From tsavah; a command, whether human or divine.

Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
The pupil (of the eye). Diminutive from 'iysh; the little man of the eye; the pupil or ball; hence, the middle.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.

A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
4: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Sister. Irregular feminine of 'ach; a sister (like 'Ezrachiy), literally and figuratively).

Kinsman. Or rather modao; from yada'; an acquaintance.

A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Foreign, alien. From neker; strange, in a variety of degrees and applications.
To be smooth, to apportion, separate. A primitive root; to be smooth; by implication to apportion or separate.

To overhang, look out or down. A primitive root; properly, to lean out, i.e. peep or gaze.
From ad with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.

Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

Needy, lacking, in want of. From chacer; lacking; hence, without.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.

A street. From shuwq; a street.
A joining together, proximity. From 'atsal; a side; near.
A corner. Feminine of pen; an angle; by implication, a pinnacle; figuratively, a chieftain.
To step, march. A primitive root; to pace, i.e. Step regularly; to mount; to march; to hurl.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

The pupil (of the eye). Diminutive from 'iysh; the little man of the eye; the pupil or ball; hence, the middle.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
10: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
To encounter, befall. A primitive root: to encounter, whether accidentally or in a hostile manner.
A garment. From shiyth; a dress.
To commit fornication, be a harlot. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to commit idolatry.
To watch, guard, keep. A primitive root; to guard, in a good sense Or a bad one.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.

Stubborn or rebellious. A primitive root; to turn away, i.e. be refractory.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

A broad open place, plaza. Or rchowb; from rachab; a width, i.e. avenue or area.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

To kiss, to equip with weapons. A primitive root; to kiss, literally or figuratively; also, to equip with weapons.
To be strong. A primitive root; to be stout.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
14: A sacrifice for alliance or friendship, peace offering. From shalam; properly, requital, i.e. A sacrifice in thanks.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
A vow. Or neder; from nadar; a promise; also a thing promised.
15: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
To encounter, befall. A primitive root: to encounter, whether accidentally or in a hostile manner.
To look early or diligently for. A primitive root; properly, to dawn, i.e. be early at any task; by extension, to search for.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.

A couch, divan. From an unused root meaning perhaps to arch; a couch.
A spread, coverlet. From rabad; a coverlet.
Dark-hued stuffs. Feminine passive participle of chatab; properly, a carving; hence, a tapestry.

Place of lying, a couch, act of lying. From shakab; a bed; abstractly, sleep; by euphemism, carnal intercourse.
Myrrh. Or mowr; from marar; myrrh.
The aloe (a tree). Or ahalowth; of foreign origin; aloe wood.
Cinnamon. From an unused root; cinnamon bark.

To be saturated, drink one's fill. A primitive root; to slake the thirst.
Or dod; from an unused root meaning properly, to boil, i.e. to love; by implication, a love- token, lover, friend; specifically an uncle.

Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.

Love (noun). From 'adash; meaning the same as 'ahab.

Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

Suddenness, suddenly. Or pith.om; from petha'; instantly.

Town, city. From qarah in the sense of building; a city.
15: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
To be smooth, straight, or right. A primitive root; to be straight or even; figuratively, to be right, pleasant, prosperous.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
16: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Hither. From hen; hither or thither.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Needy, lacking, in want of. From chacer; lacking; hence, without.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
17: To steal. A primitive root; to thieve; by implication, to deceive.

To become or be sweet or pleasant. A primitive root; to suck, by implication, to relish, or be sweet.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
To be pleasant, delightful, or lovely. A primitive root; to be agreeable.

A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.

To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
Awe-inspiring, terror-striking. From arats; fearful, i.e. Powerful or tyrannical.
A primitive root; to sustain; by implication, to obtain, keep fast; figuratively, to help, follow close.
17: Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A primitive root; to treat a person, i.e. Benefit or requite; by implication, to ripen, i.e. to wean.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To stir up, disturb, trouble. A primitive root; properly, to roil water; figuratively, to disturb or affict.
Flesh. From sha'ar; flesh, as living or for food; generally food of any kind; figuratively, kindred by blood.
18: Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Deception, disappointment, falsehood. From shaqar; an untruth; by implication, a sham.
A work, recompense. Feminine passive participle of pa'al; work.
To sow, scatter seed. A primitive root; to sow; figuratively, to disseminate, plant, fructify.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
Firmness, faithfulness, truth. Contracted from 'aman; stability; certainty, truth, trustworthiness.
19: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
20: Twisted, perverted. From aqash; distorted; hence, false.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Complete, sound. From tamam; entire; also integrity, truth.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.
21: Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
A sowing, seed, offspring. From zara'; seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity.
A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
22: Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
A ring (worn as an ornament). From an unused root of uncertain meaning; a nose-ring.
Swine, boar. From an unused root probably meaning to enclose; a hog.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Taste, judgment. From ta'am; properly, a taste, i.e. perception; by implication, intelligence; transitively, a mandate.
23: A desire. From 'avah; a longing; by implication, a delight.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
24: To scatter. A primitive root; to scatter, whether in enmity or bounty.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To withhold, refrain. A primitive root; to restrain or Refrain; by implication, to refuse, spare, preserve; also to observe.
Straightness, uprightness. From yashar; the right.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
A need, thing needed, poverty. Or machcor; from chacer; deficiency; hence, impoverishment.
25: A blessing. From barak; benediction; by implication prosperity.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
A primitive root; to be fat; transitively, to fatten; specifically to anoint; figuratively, to satisfy; denominatively to remove ashes.
To be saturated, drink one's fill. A primitive root; to slake the thirst.
Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
26: People. Or l owm; from an unused root meaning to gather; a community.
To utter a curse against, curse. A primitive root; to scoop out, i.e. to malign or execrate.
To withhold, hold back. A primitive root; to debar from benefit or injury.
Grain, corn. Or bar; from barar; grain of any kind; by extens. The open country.

Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
To buy grain. Denominative from sheber; to deal in grain.
27: To look early or diligently for. A primitive root; properly, to dawn, i.e. be early at any task; by extension, to search for.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A primitive root; properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow; by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship.
28: To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; to break forth as a bud, i.e. Bloom; generally, to spread; specifically, to fly; figuratively, to flourish.
Leaf, leafage. From alah; a leaf; collectively, foliage.
29: To stir up, disturb, trouble. A primitive root; properly, to roil water; figuratively, to disturb or affict.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
A primitive root; to inherit (figurative) mode of descent), or to occupy; causatively, to bequeath, or distribute, instate.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
30: Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
31: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.

From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.

Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.

Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
34: Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Or chattacth; from chata'; an offence, and its penalty, occasion, sacrifice, or expiation; also an offender.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
People. Or l owm; from an unused root meaning to gather; a community.
35: Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.

1: Tender, delicate, soft. From rakak; tender; by implication, weak.
An answer, response. From anah; a reply.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
An earthen vessel, toil, a pang. From atsab; an earthen vessel; usually toil; also a pang: grievous, idol, labor, sorrow.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
2: Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
To be good, well, glad, or pleasing. A primitive root; to be make well, literally or figuratively.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.
To flow, spring, bubble up. A primitive root; to gush forth; figuratively, to utter; specifically, to emit.
Folly. From the same as 'eviyl; silliness.
3: An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
A primitive root; properly, to lean forward, i.e. To peer into the distance; by implication, to observe, await.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
4: From rapha'; properly, curative, i.e. Literally a medicine, or a cure; figuratively deliverance, or placidity.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Crookedness, crooked dealing. From calaph; distortion, i.e. viciousness.
Or sheber; from shabar; a fracture, figuratively, ruin; specifically, a solution.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
5: Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
To spurn, treat with contempt. A primitive root; to scorn; or by interchange for nuwts, to bloom.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
To be shrewd or crafty. A primitive root; properly, to be bare; but used only in the derivative sense to be cunning.
6: A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Product, revenue. From bow'; income, i.e. Produce.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To stir up, disturb, trouble. A primitive root; properly, to roil water; figuratively, to disturb or affict.
7: Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
To scatter, fan, winnow. A primitive root; to toss about; by implication, to diffuse, winnow.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.
8: A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.

Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
10: Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
And towkachath; from yakach; chastisement; figuratively correction, refutation, proof.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
11: Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
(place of) destruction or ruin, Abaddon. Intensive from 'abad; abstract, a perishing; concrete, Hades.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
Heart. Feminine of leb; the heart.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
12: A primitive root; properly, to make mouths at, i.e. To scoff; hence to interpret, or intercede.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
13: Glad, joyful, merry. From samach; blithe or gleeful.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To be good, well, glad, or pleasing. A primitive root; to be make well, literally or figuratively.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A hurt, injury, pain. From atsab; a idol; also, a pain or wound.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
Smitten, afflicted. Or nakat; from naka'; smitten, i.e. afflicted.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
14: To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
Folly. From the same as 'eviyl; silliness.
15: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
A feast, drink. From shathah; drink, by implication, drinking; also a banquet or feast.
16: A little, fewness, a few. Or miat; from ma'at; a little or few.
A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Treasure, store, a treasury, storehouse. From 'atsar; a depository.
Tumult, confusion, disquietude, discomfiture. From huwm; confusion or uproar.
17: A meal, allowance. Feminine passive participle of 'arach; a ration of food.
Herbs, herbage. From the same as yereq; properly, green; concretely, a vegetable.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
Love (noun). Feminine of 'ahab and meaning the same.
To feed, fatten. A primitive root; to fodder.
A head of cattle (bullock, ox, etc.). From shuwr; a bullock.
18: Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To stir up (strife), engage in strife. A primitive root; properly, to grate, i.e. to anger.
Strife, contention. From diyn; a contest or quarrel.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To be quiet or undisturbed. A primitive root; to repose.
Strife, dispute. Or rib; from riyb; a contest.
19: Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A hedge. Or msukah; from suwk; a hedge.
A briar. From an unused root meaning to sting; a prickly plant.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
To lift up, cast up. A primitive root; to mound up; figurative, to exalt; reflexively, to oppose.
20: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.

Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
Needy, lacking, in want of. From chacer; lacking; hence, without.

Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.

To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
To be smooth, straight, or right. A primitive root; to be straight or even; figuratively, to be right, pleasant, prosperous.
22: Thought, device. Or machashebeth; from chashab; a contrivance, i.e. a texture, machine, or intention, plan.
To break up, to violate, frustrate. A primitive root; to break up
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Council, counsel. From yacad; a session, i.e. Company of persons; by implication, intimacy, consultation, a secret.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
To advise, counsel. A primitive root; to advise; reflexively, to deliberate or resolve.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.

Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.

Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.

Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.

A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Proud. From ga'ah; lofty; figuratively, arrogant.
To take one's stand, stand. A prim root; to station, in various applications.
Border, boundary, territory. Or gbul; from gabal; properly, a cord, i.e. a boundary; by extens. The territory inclosed.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.

Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Clean, pure. Or tahor; from taher; pure.
Delightfulness, pleasantness. From na'em; agreeableness, i.e. Delight, suitableness, splendor or grace.

A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
12: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
Seat of honor, throne. Or kicceh; from kacah; properly, covered, i.e. A throne.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
13: Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
14: Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
A messenger. From an unused root meaning to despatch as a deputy; a messenger; specifically, of God, i.e. An angel.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to expiate or condone, to placate or cancel.
15: Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Goodwill, favor, acceptance, will. Or ratson; from ratsah; delight.
Latter rain, spring rain. From laqash; the spring rain; figuratively, eloquence.
16: A primitive root; to erect, i.e. Create; by extension, to procure, especially by purchase; by implication to own.
Incised, incisive, a trench, gold, a threshing-sledge, determination, eager.
A primitive root; to erect, i.e. Create; by extension, to procure, especially by purchase; by implication to own.
To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
17: A highway. From calal; a thoroughfare, literally or figuratively; specifically a viaduct, a staircase.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To watch, guard, keep. A primitive root; to guard, in a good sense Or a bad one.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
18: Arrogance, majesty, ornament. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.

Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

A stumbling. From kashal; properly, a tottering, i.e. Ruin.

Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
To be smooth, to apportion, separate. A primitive root; to be smooth; by implication to apportion or separate.

With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
Proud. From ga'ah; lofty; figuratively, arrogant.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
21: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
A learning, teaching. From laqach; properly, something received, i.e. instruction; also inveiglement.
22: Prudence, insight. Or sekel; from sakal; intelligence; by implication, success.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Owner, lord. From ba'al; a master; hence, a husband, or owner.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
Folly. From the same as 'eviyl; silliness.

(honey) comb. From tsuwph; comb of honey.

Sweet, sweetness. Or mathuwq; from mathaq; sweet.

From rapha'; properly, curative, i.e. Literally a medicine, or a cure; figuratively deliverance, or placidity.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.

Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

A crown, wreath. From atar; a crown.

Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Crooked. By reduplication from haphak; very perverse.
Doing, deed, work. From pa'al; an act or work.
9: A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A corner. Feminine of pen; an angle; by implication, a pinnacle; figuratively, a chieftain.
A roof, a top. Probably by reduplication from ga'ah; a roof; by analogy, the top of an altar.
Company, association, spell. From chabar; a society; also a spell.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Brawling, contention. A variation for madown.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
10: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To incline, desire. A primitive root; to wish for.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Friend, companion, fellow. Or reya2; from ra'ah; an associate.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
11: A primitive root; properly, to make mouths at, i.e. To scoff; hence to interpret, or intercede.
To fine, mulct. A primitive root; properly, to urge; by implication, to inflict a penalty, specifically, to fine.
Simple, perhaps open-minded. Or pethiy; or pthaeiy; from pathah; silly.
To be wise. A primitive root, to be wise.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
12: To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To twist, pervert, overturn. A primitive root; properly, to wrench, i.e. to subvert.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
13: To shut, shut up. A primitive root; to close; by analology to contract.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
A cry, outcry. And zoaqah; from za'aq; a shriek or outcry.
Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
14: A gift. From nathan; a present.
A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
To subdue. A primitive root; properly, to bend, i.e. to tame or subdue.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Bosom. Or cheq; and chowq; from an unused root, apparently meaning to inclose; the bosom.
A present, bribe. From shachad; a donation.
Strong, mighty, fierce. From azaz; strong, vehement, harsh.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
15: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
Terror, destruction, ruin. From chathah; properly, a dissolution; concretely, a ruin, or consternation.
Do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, especially to practise.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
16: Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To err. A primitive root; to vacillate, i.e. Reel or stray; also causative of both.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
Assembly, convocation, congregation. From qahal; assemblage.
Shades, ghosts. From rapha' in the sense of raphah; properly, lax, i.e. a ghost.
17: To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
A need, thing needed, poverty. Or machcor; from chacer; deficiency; hence, impoverishment.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
Wine. From an unused root meaning to effervesce; wine; by implication, intoxication.
Fat, oil. From shaman; grease, especially liquid; figuratively, richness.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be or become rich. A primitive root; properly, to accumulate; chiefly to grow rich.
18: Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
From kaphar; properly, a cover, i.e. a village; bitumen, and the henna plant; figuratively, a redemption-price.
To act or deal treacherously. A primitive root; to cover; figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
19: A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
Strife, contention. From diyn; a contest or quarrel.
Vexation, anger. Or kaoas; from ka'ac; vexation.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
20: To desire, take pleasure in. A primitive root; to delight in.
Treasure, store, a treasury, storehouse. From 'atsar; a depository.
Fat, oil. From shaman; grease, especially liquid; figuratively, richness.
Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.
Stupid fellow, dullard, fool. From kacal; properly, fat, i.e. stupid or silly.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To swallow down, swallow up, engulf. A primitive root; to make away with; generally, to destroy.
21: To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
22: A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
To come or go down, descend.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
Confidence. From batach; properly, a refuge, i.e. security, or assurance.
23: To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
24: A primitive root; properly, to make mouths at, i.e. To scoff; hence to interpret, or intercede.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Proud, haughty. Probably from the same as har; elated; hence, arrogant.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
25: A desire. From 'avah; a longing; by implication, a delight.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
To refuse. A primitive root; to refuse.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
26: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To incline, desire. A primitive root; to wish for.
A desire. From 'avah; a longing; by implication, a delight.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To withhold, refrain. A primitive root; to restrain or Refrain; by implication, to refuse, spare, preserve; also to observe.
27: A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A plan, device, wickedness. Or zammah; from zamam; a plan, especially a bad one.
28: A lie, falsehood, deceptive thing. From kazab; falsehood; literally or figuratively.
A witness. Contracted from uwd; concretely, a witness; abstractly, testimony; specifically, a recorder, i.e. Prince.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
29: Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To be strong. A primitive root; to be stout.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.

An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.

Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

Deliverance, salvation. Or tshuah; from shava' in the sense of yasha'; rescue.

Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Natron or carbonate of soda. From nathar; mineral potash.
To sing. Or shuwr; a primitive root; to sing.
A song, singing. Or feminine shiyrah; from shiyr; a song; abstractly, singing.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
21: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Cause to drink water, give to drink. A primitive root; to quaff, i.e. to irrigate or furnish a potion to.
22: To snatch up. A primitive root; to lay hold of; especially to pick up fire.
Coal. Or gacheleth; from an unused root meaning to glow or kindle; an ember.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
